summary: Market sentiment is cautiously mixed, oscillating between risk-off anxiety driven by the ongoing US-Iran conflict and episodic relief rallies whenever ceasefire or deal reports surface, leaving investors in a state of heightened uncertainty. The dominant macro driver is the US-Iran war, which is simultaneously pushing US inflation higher, disrupting global jet fuel and energy trade routes, and prompting Washington to shut down Iranian airlines' access to landing, refueling, and ticketing infrastructure, while a tentative ceasefire framework reportedly awaits final approval from President Trump. On the sector and stock front, Snowflake surged approximately 35% in its best day ever on AI-driven enthusiasm, sparking a broader software rally, while Starbucks offered a rare consumer bright spot as afternoon traffic data signaled early turnaround traction, and Costco's upcoming earnings are being closely scrutinized for consumer spending health metrics. The primary risk factors commanding investor attention include the fragility of the US-Iran ceasefire — with reports of it unravelling even as a deal outline emerges — escalating regional spillover evidenced by Kuwait intercepting missiles and drones, persistently firm US inflation complicating Fed policy, and volatile crude oil prices disrupting energy and shipping markets globally. In the short term, markets are likely to remain headline-driven and volatile, with crude oil, gold, and the dollar acting as real-time barometers of geopolitical progress, while selective technology strength in AI names may provide partial offsetting support to broader equity indices.
